text.title: Statistical Significance: Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Statistical Significance: Basics` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Alpha (significance level) MUST be set before the test starts. Standard alpha = 0.05 unless multi-test correction applies.
rule.rationale: Setting alpha post-hoc is a fishing license. Pre-commit alpha = pre-commit to honest stopping rule.

rule.statement: Sample size per arm MUST come from power analysis (baseline rate + MDE + alpha + 80% power). Hand-wave sample sizes forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without power analysis, an inconclusive test could mean 'no effect' or 'too small to detect'. Power analysis tells you what sample size is honest for the question.

rule.statement: Null + alternative hypothesis MUST be written out before the test (H0: control = treatment; H1: control != treatment OR pre-registered direction).
rule.rationale: Vague hypothesis = vague test = post-hoc rationalization. Formal H0 + H1 keeps the test honest.

rule.statement: Test design MUST acknowledge Type I (alpha, false-positive) vs Type II (1-power, false-negative) tradeoff. Default 80% power = 20% Type II rate.

rationale.source: Cohen 1988; Practical Statistics for Data Scientists

rule.rationale: Type II error is the silent killer — you keep the bad treatment because the test was too small to see the effect. Explicit acknowledgment forces honest sample-size discussion.

rule.statement: Default frequentist test MUST not peek + stop. If peeking required, use sequential testing (mSPRT, alpha-spending, Bayesian) — declared in pre-test brief.

rationale.source: Sequential testing literature; Berman / Optimizely guides

rule.rationale: Peeking inflates actual alpha. The fix is either fixed-horizon (no peeking) or explicit sequential method. Mixing the two = false positives.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies If fail OR any item in Skip If triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
